3 min read
Just now

--

Let’s get something straight right away. Google Ads are not the only way to appear on Google. Paid ads are one lane on the motorway. SEO is the lane next to it, and it’s free. You just have to build the road yourself.
Most small business owners either don’t know this or assume it’s too technical. It’s not. Here’s exactly how to do it, step by step, without spending a cent on ads.
What is SEO, actually?
SEO stands for Search Engine Optimisation. It’s the practice of making your website show up when people search for what you offer. When someone types “best coffee shop in Thessaloniki” or “web designer near me” or “physiotherapist Athens” into Google, the results that appear are there because of SEO.
The sites at the top didn’t pay for those positions. They earned them. And you can too.
Step 1: Set up your Google Business Profile
This is free and it’s the single most impactful thing you can do for local SEO. Go to business.google.com, claim your business, add your address, phone number, website, opening hours, and photos.
Once your profile is live, you can appear in the Map Pack, those three businesses that show up with a map when someone searches locally. That is incredibly valuable real estate and it costs nothing except your time.
“The Map Pack gets clicked more than the regular results. Being in it can double your enquiries overnight.”
Step 2: Collect Google reviews
Google uses reviews as a trust signal. More reviews, higher rating, better ranking. It’s that simple. The way to get them is equally simple: ask your clients directly. Send them a message after you finish a job. Most people are happy to leave a review if you make it easy, share the direct link so they don’t have to go looking for it.
Ten genuine 5-star reviews will do more for your local ranking than almost anything else.
Step 3: Write content your customers are searching for
This is where blogs earn their keep. Every question your clients ask you is a blog post waiting to be written. “How much does a website cost?” Write it. “What’s the difference between a landing page and a full website?” Write it. “How long does it take to build a website?” Write it.
When Google sees your site consistently answering questions your target audience is asking, it starts treating you as an authority on the topic. Slowly your pages move up. Traffic builds. Enquiries come in.
Step 4: Make sure your site is fast and mobile-friendly
Google openly penalises slow sites in its rankings. If your website takes more than 3 seconds to load on a phone, you’re losing visitors before they even read a word, and Google notices that.
You can test your site speed for free at pagespeed.web.dev. Aim for a green score of 90+ on mobile. A properly built static website, like the ones we build, scores well here without any extra effort.
Step 5: Use the right words on your pages
If you’re a florist in Heraklion, the words “florist”, “flowers”, and “Heraklion” need to appear naturally on your homepage, your about page, and your service pages. Not stuffed in unnaturally, just used the way you’d naturally talk about your business.
Think about what your ideal customer types into Google and make sure those exact phrases appear somewhere on your site.
How long does it take?
Honest answer: 3 to 6 months to see real results. SEO is a long game. But unlike ads, it doesn’t stop working the moment you stop paying. A well-optimised page can send you free traffic for years.
Start now. The sooner you do, the sooner it pays off.
